comment: AI review by Greptile — focus: correctness, safety, API stability
instructions: Rust workspace (11 crates). Focus on: memory safety, async correctness (Tokio), public API semver stability, and panic paths (unwrap/expect outside tests). Do not flag formatting — rustfmt + clippy handle it. This is a push-based AI memory kernel; correctness of embedding math, LSH bucket hashing, Hebbian decay, and FSRS scheduling is high priority.

crates/shrimpk-core/**.instructions: Shared type definitions. Any public API change risks breaking semver. Flag without mercy.

crates/shrimpk-memory/**.instructions: Main engine. Flag: RwLock held across await, incorrect float comparisons, off-by-one in LSH/FSRS math, panics.

crates/shrimpk-daemon/**.instructions: axum server. Check: error handler coverage, response shape consistency, missing auth.

crates/shrimpk-mcp/**.instructions: MCP stdio server. Flag JSON-RPC protocol violations and panics.

crates/shrimpk-context/**.instructions: Token-budgeted context assembly. Flag off-by-one token counts and budget overflow.

crates/shrimpk-python/**.instructions: PyO3 bindings. All Results must map to PyErr — flag any unwrap() or panic path.

tests/**.instructions: Test files only. Flag logic bugs causing false results; ignore style.

crates/shrimpk-ros2/**.instructions: Stub crate. Flag only panics or compilation errors.

crates/shrimpk-security/**.instructions: PII stub. Flag only incorrect regex patterns that miss PII.

rules.rule: Do not use unwrap() or expect() outside of #[cfg(test)] blocks
Do not hold RwLock or Mutex guards across an .await point
Public types in shrimpk-core must derive Serialize and Deserialize consistently — flag missing impls
New public APIs require a corresponding integration test

other.context: Apache 2.0 Rust workspace. shrimpk-core=shared types, shrimpk-memory=EchoEngine+embedder+LSH+Hebbian+FSRS, shrimpk-daemon=axum HTTP, shrimpk-mcp=MCP stdio, shrimpk-context=token-budgeted prompts, shrimpk-router=cascade routing, shrimpk-python=PyO3, shrimpk-ros2=stub, shrimpk-kernel=facade re-export.
Benchmarks use criterion. Benchmark harness boilerplate (black_box, criterion_group!, criterion_main!) is intentional — do not flag as dead code or unused imports.
